title: PRESCHOOL PROMISE - 211info
text: MARION AND POLK EARLY LEARNING HUB PRESCHOOL PROMISE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps locate publicly funded preschools in family care, center, and school settings. Eligible students are matched to sites based on family preference and need. Setting options include days, hours of operation, transportation, primary language, bilingual programs, curriculum, and approach. 2995 Ryan Drive SE Suite 100 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -Must be ages 3-4 before September 1st of the calendar year -Household income must be below 200% federal poverty level or children in foster care -Must be an Oregon resident -Age 4 on or before September 10 regardless of income if the child resides if the following school boundaries: Bush Elementary, Four Corners Elementary, Highland Elementary, Grant Elementary, Scott Elementary, Swegle Elementary, Richmond Elementary, and Washington Elementary -Families receiving SNAP do not need to provide additional income verification -Housing Adjustment: Eligibility Specialists may adjust a family’s gross income calculation when determining eligibility to account for excessive housing costs. This applies to families who do not meet income eligibility but spend more than 30% of their gross income on housing costs Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 701-0438 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 701-0438 Katrina Casas [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website to apply or for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of income, proof of address LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Marion and Polk Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Child Care Expense Assistance * Foster Children Child Care Expense Assistance * Preschool Age Children Preschool Referral Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - MARION AND POLK EARLY LEARNING HUB INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - LANTERN INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ODHS FAMILY VISITATION SUPPORT PUBLIC SAFETY - CAR SEAT PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
